answer.text: A government regulator of mutual funds
Association of Mutual Funds in India - an industry body representing mutual fund houses
A mutual fund scheme run by the government
A stock exchange for mutual fund trading

explanation.text: **The Logic:** AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India) is the industry association of all SEBI-registered mutual funds in India. It is a self-regulatory organization (SRO) that works to promote the mutual fund industry, maintain ethical standards, and protect investor interests. AMFI also registers mutual fund distributors and issues ARN (AMFI Registration Number) to qualified distributors. **The Trap:** AMFI is NOT a government body or regulator. It is an industry association. SEBI is the actual regulator. AMFI's role is to promote best practices, create a code of conduct, and facilitate the growth of the mutual fund industry. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A and Series XXIV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Every mutual fund distributor must register with AMFI and obtain an ARN number. This ARN must be disclosed to investors in all transactions. AMFI's Code of Conduct for Intermediaries sets the ethical standards that distributors must follow.
